microwave_time.user_tendencies: IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND neutral AND refrigerated savory meal THEN 2 min BECAUSE they prefer food served warm without overcooking it. IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND physically fatigued AND refrigerated savory meal THEN 3 min BECAUSE fatigue makes hotter food and fewer reheating complaints preferable. IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND in a hurry AND refrigerated savory meal THEN 1 min BECAUSE speed matters more than ideal temperature. IF any dining context AND neutral AND fruit or cold dessert THEN no microwave BECAUSE those foods are intended to be served cold. IF social setting with a partner on either side AND neutral AND refrigerated savory meal THEN 2 min BECAUSE a standard warm temperature is socially safe and predictable. IF watching TV or working in front AND relaxed with ample time AND refrigerated savory meal THEN 3 min BECAUSE they can tolerate extra prep time for a hotter meal. IF social setting AND physically fatigued AND refrigerated savory meal THEN 3 min BECAUSE this user prioritizes reducing later effort and dissatisfaction under fatigue.

occlusion_relevance.user_tendencies: IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND neutral AND foods without sauces THEN do not consider occlusion BECAUSE there is no competing visual target. IF social setting with a person to the left AND neutral AND any food THEN minimize left occlusion BECAUSE they want to preserve sightlines to the social partner. IF social setting with a person to the right AND neutral AND any food THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE they want to preserve that interaction. IF watching TV in front AND neutral AND any food THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE the screen is the main competing focus. IF working on a laptop or tablet in front AND neutral AND any food THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE task visibility matters. IF social setting on one side AND physically fatigued AND messy or saucy foods THEN minimize left occlusion BECAUSE this user keeps social visibility priorities even when fatigue adds other demands. IF watching TV or working in front AND in a hurry AND any food THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE efficiency should not interrupt the primary visual activity.

robot_speed.user_tendencies: IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND neutral AND foods without sauces THEN medium BECAUSE they like efficient but not abrupt motion. IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND in a hurry AND easy foods or drinks THEN fast BECAUSE they are comfortable with quicker service. IF any dining context AND physically fatigued AND any food THEN medium BECAUSE fatigue increases desire for low effort but they still avoid overly abrupt motion near the face. IF social setting with a partner AND neutral AND any food THEN medium BECAUSE moderate speed feels socially acceptable and controlled. IF social setting with a partner AND physically fatigued AND any food THEN medium BECAUSE this user prioritizes low effort under fatigue but not at the cost of comfort. IF watching TV or working in front AND in a hurry AND any food THEN fast BECAUSE they want the meal to interfere less with the ongoing activity. IF difficult bite acquisition foods like meat pieces AND neutral AND feeding task THEN medium BECAUSE reliability matters more than maximum speed.

skewering_axis.user_tendencies: IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND neutral AND elongated or larger solid foods THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE limited mouth opening makes narrower bites easier. IF any dining context AND neutral AND tough meats with repeated acquisition failures THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE acquisition reliability can override bite narrowness after failures. IF social setting with a partner AND neutral AND elongated foods THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE avoiding oversized awkward bites is socially preferable. IF eating alone AND physically fatigued AND elongated foods THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE smaller bites reduce effort during transfer and chewing. IF any dining context AND in a hurry AND bite-sized compact foods THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE faster reliable pickup matters when mouth-opening constraints are less relevant. IF watching TV or working in front AND neutral AND foods likely to rotate or slip THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE stable acquisition reduces interruptions. IF social setting AND physically fatigued AND elongated foods with moderate acquisition difficulty THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE this user prioritizes ease of eating over maximum pickup success when fatigued.
